author: gb9t
content: Iteration 40: vhost/vhost-core.md committed (fd38a83, 213 lines). vhost framework — kernel-side virtio backends. Used by every modern cloud KVM (vhost-net eliminates QEMU userspace round-trip per packet), SPDK/nvme-tcp/iSCSI (vhost-scsi), Firecracker/Cloud-Hypervisor (vhost-vsock), vDPA hw acceleration (vhost-vdpa). ~13K LOC. Three-way trust split: guest + QEMU userspace + kernel. Defenses: VRING addr validation strict (closes CVE-2023-39189), IOTLB msg validation (closes 'compromised QEMU sends bogus IOTLB → vhost OOB'), backend fd type validation (closes type-confusion), per-vq access serialized (closes CVE-2024-26818 config-change race), workqueue cgroup-confined (closes container-escape via root-cgroup), vsock CID per-namespace scope (closes container-escape via shared CID), zero-copy TX page validation (closes 'zero-copy points to host page → guest reads host data'). Virtualization stack now complete: IOMMU + VFIO + virtio + vhost. 40 docs / ~2.13M LOC. Next iteration: drivers/dma-buf/ (cross-driver buffer sharing — used by GPUDirect, V4L2, VFIO, mlx5_ib + every modern userspace GPU/video pipeline).
